---
name: Changelog request
about: I expected this change to be listed, but it isn't
title: "[changelog] "
labels: changelog
assignees: ''
---

## What change did you observe?

Describe the behaviour change you noticed in Veripsa's check, comment, or
dashboard. What did Veripsa do before, and what is it doing now?

> Example: "Until Tuesday, the check on my repo would list overlapping PRs in
> the suggested land order. As of Wednesday it stopped doing that on PRs
> that touch a renamed file."

## When did you first notice it?

A date (or a date range) is enough. We group changelog entries by the date
of deploy to production, so we'll match your observation against our deploy
log.

## Which repository (optional)?

If you're comfortable sharing the repo (or just the org), it helps us
correlate. **Do not paste private code, file paths, or commit messages** —
we don't need them and we don't want to receive them.

## What you'd like

- [ ] Add an entry to the changelog covering this change.
- [ ] Tell me this was an internal change with no customer-visible effect.
- [ ] Tell me this was intentional and direct me to where it's documented.

## Anything else?

(Optional.) Anything that would help us locate the change — a screenshot of
the check comment, the URL of the PR check (Veripsa team can see it), etc.

---

**Please do not include:**

- Source code or file contents from your repo.
- Internal commit messages from your repo.
- Customer names or third-party identifiers.

We only need to know what Veripsa's surfaces showed you.
